data.title: U.K. Manufacturing Weakness Could Continue to Drag on GDP Growth — Market Talk
data.description: The volatility of monthly GDP data means the U.K. economy was likely to struggle to follow the large 0.4% on-month jump in December, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says. Indeed, on a three-month on three-month basis, GDP climbed 0.2% compared to the 0.1% fall in January alone. Contraction in the manufacturing sector dragged GDP down by 0.1 percentage point, explaining the entire fall. But the industrial struggle was a disappointment given that eurozone industrial output rose in the same month, and PMI surveys suggest this trend could worsen in the U.K., Wishart notes. The export-intensive industrial sector marked a contrast with increasing momentum in services, highlighting a divergence between robust domestic demand but weak exports as the U.K. struggles to compete in international markets, he says. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

data.title: Stocks rise on China boost, but face weekly dip on trade war concerns
data.description:
Key points:
  • Polish stocks at highest level since 2011
  • Czech stocks hit record highs
  • Mozambique's dollar bond rises after U.S. clears $5 bln LNG loan

Emerging market stocks advanced on Friday, buoyed by heavyweight Chinese shares, but were set for modest weekly declines as U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff threats kept risk-taking in check.

MSCI's index for emerging market stocks CBOE:EFS climbed 0.9% by 0904 GMT, boosted by a more than 2% jump in both Mainland China and Hong Kong .

Despite Friday's gains, the index was on track to fall by more than 1% for the week as jitters over the Trump administration's tariff threats and fears of a U.S. economic slowdown hit risk assets globally.

In its latest trade war volley, the United States on Thursday threatened to slap a 200% tariff on wine, cognac and other alcohol imports from Europe.

U.S. Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer, meanwhile, said on Thursday he would vote to advance a Republican stopgap funding bill, signalling that his party would provide the votes to avert a government shutdown.

Equities in emerging Europe ticked higher, with Poland's blue-chip index hitting its highest level since August 2011, while Czech shares rose almost 1% to an all-time high.

Central and Eastern European currencies were slightly weaker, with Hungary's forint slipping 0.3%.

Emerging market stocks have outperformed the U.S. S&P 500 so far in March, as beaten-down Indian equities have recouped some of their losses and China has vowed more support for its ailing economy.

Central and Eastern Europe were not far behind as hopes of a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine lifted sentiment, in contrast to a sharp drop in American equities, where the S&P 500 slipped into a technical correction.

President Vladimir Putin said on Thursday that Russia supported a U.S. proposal for a ceasefire in Ukraine in principle, but that fighting could not be paused until a number of crucial conditions were worked out or clarified.

"The move lower in U.S. equity markets has been unsettling, and markets are concerned we're heading into a bear market rather than a mere correction," Barclays analysts said in a note.

MSCI's index for emerging market currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) has gained around 1.5% so far this year while the dollar has sagged globally.

A big mover on Friday was Mozambique's sovereign dollar bond (XS2051203946=TE), which gained just under 2 cents, after the United States cleared a long-delayed $5 billion loan for a liquefied natural gas project.

South Africa's rand firmed 0.5% against the dollar after declining earlier this week when Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana presented his revised national budget.

Godongwana will engage with lawmakers about the budget later in the day.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Polish cenbank's Kotecki sees rate cut proposal by September 'at latest'

** Hungary supports common European defence policy but not joint borrowing, PM Orban says

For TOP NEWS across emerging markets

data.title: Correction: Docusign Fiscal Q4 Earnings, Revenue Rise; Fiscal Q1, 2026 Guidance Set -- Shares Rise
data.description:
(Corrects revenue guidance for fiscal 2026 in the headline and fifth paragraph.)

Docusign late Thursday reported fiscal Q4 non-GAAP net income of $0.86 per diluted share, up from $0.76 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet, on average, expected $0.85.

Revenue for the quarter ended Jan. 31 was $776.3 million, compared with $712.4 million a year earlier. Analysts expected $761.2 million.

For Q1, the electronic signature company is projecting revenue in a range of $745 million to $749 million. The consensus is $756.8 million.

For fiscal 2026, Docusign said it expects revenue to be $3.13 billion to $3.14 billion. Analysts are looking for $3.15 billion.

Docusign shares recently were up over 10% in recent premarket trading.

data.description:
On March 14, 2025, Nordnet AB (publ), through its subsidiary NordnetLivsforsikring AS, net bought 456,955 shares in Nordic Financials ASA (ISINNO0012958539). After these transactions, Nordnet AB (publ), through itssubsidiaries, held a total of 1,626,503 shares in the company on aconsolidatedbasis, representing 6.84% of the outstanding shares and votes in the company.

The ownership distribution among Nordnet AB (publ) subsidiaries is as follows: Nordnet Pensionsförsäkring AB: 0.180%Nordnet Livsforsikring AS: 6.656%

Nordnet AB (publ) does not exercise voting rights for these shares. This disclosure is made in accordance with sections 4-2 and 4-3 of theNorwegianSecurities Trading Act and is subject to the disclosure requirement pursuanttosection 5-12 of the same Act.

data.title: Li Auto Profit Drops Despite Record Revenue
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

Chinese plug-in hybrid specialist Li Auto reported lower net profit for the fourth quarter despite record revenue as a brutal price war hurt its bottom line.

The Beijing-based carmaker said Friday that its net profit fell 38% to 3.52 billion yuan, equivalent to $485.9 million. That beat the 3.22 billion yuan estimate of analysts in a Visible Alpha poll.

Revenue for the period rose 6.1% to 44.27 billion yuan, which the company said was a new quarterly record, topping analysts' estimate of 43.26 billion yuan.

Its gross margin fell to 20.3% from 23.5% a year ago and was lower than 21.5% in the third quarter, it said. The company said the year-over-year margin weakness was due to product-mix changes. It attributed the sequential drop to losses on purchase commitment and lower selling prices due to interest subsidies provided to customers.

Li Auto maintained its margin above 20%, however, thanks to higher-priced models accounting for a greater share of sales volume and its solid progress in autonomous-driving technology. The EV maker sold 158,696 vehicles during the period, up from the previous quarter but slightly missing its delivery guidance.

For the first quarter, the company said it expects to deliver between 88,000 and 93,000 vehicles and post revenue of 23.4 billion yuan to 24.7 billion yuan.

Known for its premium plug-in hybrids, Li Auto stands out as one of the few profitable Chinese EV makers. For 2024, its net profit fell 31% to 8.03 billion yuan and gross margin slipped to 20.5%. That compared with U.S. rival Tesla's 17.9% margin for the year. Year to date, Nasdaq-listed Li Auto has climbed 20%, while Tesla's stock has slumped 40%.

The Chinese automaker's American depositary receipts fell about 3.3% in premarket trading after the results.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Timee Logs 1.31 Billion Yen Profit in Q3 on Strong Labor Demand
data.description:
Timee reported a profit attributable to owners of the parent of 1.31 billion yen for the three months ended Jan. 31, according to a Tokyo bourse filing on Wednesday.

The media company's earnings per share (EPS) stood at 12.29 yen and net sales came in at 8.64 billion yen.

Comparison figures for the previous year were not provided.

For the fiscal year ending Oct. 31, Timee expects net sales to range between 34.39 billion yen and 35.70 billion yen, representing a growth of up to 32.8%.

Attributable profit is forecast to be in the range of 4.38 billion yen to 4.93 billion yen, with EPS projected between 45.12 yen and 50.78 yen.

The company does not plan to pay dividends for the fiscal year.

data.title: Ronshine Service to Delist on March 18 After Court Sanctions Privatization Scheme
data.description:
Ronshine Service Holding said the Grand Court of the Cayman Islands sanctioned its privatization scheme as well as capital reduction on March 12, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The scheme of arrangement became effective today, March 14, while the withdrawal of listing of the company's shares will take place on March 18.

Rongxin Yipin has offered to take Ronshine Service private in January via a scheme of arrangement. The offeror is wholly owned by Rongan Juxiang, one of the controlling shareholders of the company.

data.title: ZhongAn Online Logs 5.03 Billion Yuan in January-February Gross Written Premiums
data.description: Insurer ZhongAn Online P & C Insurance recorded gross written premiums of around 5.03 billion yuan for the two months ended Feb. 28, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

data.title: XRP News: Why XRP Is No Longer Just About Remittances, BlocScale Launchpad Is Bringing Startups, and Institutional Investors - Join $BLOC Seed Sale
data.description:
LIJA, Malta, March 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — For years, XRP Ledger has been recognized as a leading blockchain for cross-border payments and remittances, thanks to its speed, low transaction costs, and efficiency. However, XRP is evolving beyond its initial purpose, transforming into a hub for innovation, decentralized finance (DeFi), and real-world asset tokenization.

After a fully sold-out private sale, BlocScale has now launched its $BLOC Seed Sale, offering early investors a chance to participate in XRP’s first-ever decentralized fundraising ecosystem before broader exchange listings.

With startups, institutional investors, and DeFi projects flocking to XRP, BlocScale is leading the charge in reshaping XRPL’s future. From Payments to Innovation: XRP’s Evolution Has Begun

BlocScale Launchpad is solving this problem by:

  • Providing a structured platform for startups to raise capital on XRPL.
  • Offering decentralized fundraising without tier systems or restrictions.
  • Attracting institutional investors seeking exposure to XRP-based projects.
  • Bridging traditional businesses with blockchain through real-world asset tokenization.

The introduction of BlocScale Launchpad means that XRPL is now open for business, ready to compete with the biggest blockchain ecosystems in the world.

JOIN BLOCSCALE SEED SALE

BlocScale Launchpad: The Key to Startups and Institutional Capital on XRP Ledger

BlocScale is not just another launchpad, it is a fully integrated ecosystem designed to help startups, enterprises, and investors seamlessly participate in XRPL’s growing DeFi space.

With automated trustlines, instant liquidity, and strong marketing support, BlocScale ensures that every project launching on XRP has the best possible start.

This has already attracted attention from venture capital firms and institutional investors, as BlocScale removes the fundraising barriers that previously prevented startups from choosing XRPL.

Through $BLOC, the governance and utility token of BlocScale, investors can participate in project launches, vote on governance proposals, and fuel fundraising across the ecosystem.

$BLOC Seed Sale Details:

Total Allocation: 10,000,000 BLOC

Soft Cap: 50,000 XRP

Min Buy: 200 XRP

Max Buy: 20,000 XRP

Price: 1 XRP = 50 $BLOC

Join BlocScale’s $BLOC Seed Sale Now and become part of XRP’s growing ecosystem: https://www.blocscale.com/blocsale

By investing in $BLOC, you gain early exposure to the most significant fundraising innovation on XRPL, ensuring you benefit from every project that launches through BlocScale.

Here’s what’s next for XRP and BlocScale:

More Projects Onboarding – Web3 startups, real-world businesses, and DeFi projects are lining up to launch.

Marketing & Institutional Adoption – Increased partnerships, influencer campaigns, and VC involvement.

Real-World Asset (RWA) Tokenization – BlocScale will help businesses tokenize assets like real estate and equity on XRP Ledger.

The future of XRP is no longer just about payments, it’s about innovation, startups, and tokenized capital.

Final Thoughts: BlocScale Is Just Getting Started

With a fully sold-out private sale, a growing investor base, and real-world adoption accelerating, $BLOC is poised to become one of XRP’s most influential assets.

Invest in BlocScale’s $BLOC Seed Sale Today! Join Now: https://www.blocscale.com/blocsale

data.title: BMW Expects Flat Earnings This Year as Headwinds Continue — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

BMW expects to report slight sales growth this year but said earnings would remain flat as it faces headwinds from continuing challenges in China and costs related to tariffs.

European automakers are contending with a demand shift in China as consumers in the world's largest car market move toward local brands that are offering deep discounts, while a struggling Chinese economy has also forced a downturn in luxury spending.

The dynamics have hit higher-end German manufacturers like BMW especially hard as it derives around a third of group earnings from the country.

Competition continues to increase in China and BMW expects consumers to continue opting for lower-priced vehicles this year.

Continuing weakness in electric-vehicle demand and an escalating global trade war with tariffs on cars imported to the U.S. is also adding to uncertainty in the industry.

The company is aiming for a profit before taxes this year at the same level as 2024 but cautioned that the margin in its automotive unit will remain depressed at between 5% and 7%. It usually aims for a margin above 8%.

Shares fell 2% in early European trade.

The margin is facing pressure from tariffs and the company said duties already put in place will shave around one percentage point off the margin, assuming they remain in place all year.

The U.S. this week slapped duties on imported steel and aluminum and has also implemented a 25% levy on Canada and Mexico relating to cars that don't meet the rules of the North American free-trade pact--which requires the models to have a majority of their parts sourced within the region.

On a call with reporters, BMW executives said the company will use its global production network to change the cars that it produces at plants and alter volumes to mitigate potential tariffs as much as possible. It also sees potential for more U.S. localization of component manufacturing, which it will now analyze more thoroughly.

The Mexico and Canada tariffs will have a mid-three-digit million impact on results this year, while the steel and aluminum duties will have a high-double-digit million negative impact on earnings if they remain in place all year, it said.

Tariff increases imposed by the U.S. on imports from China and the Chinese government's countermeasures will hit earnings in the low-three-digit million range.

BMW told U.S. dealers in a message Wednesday that it will absorb the added cost of new tariffs on its imports from Mexico--at least for the next several weeks.

Speaking on the media call, Chief Financial Officer Walter Mertl said that should the U.S. increase the tariff on imported cars from the EU to 10% from the current 2.5%, it would hit the margin by a further half a percentage point if the tariff remained in place all year.

BMW has spent big on refreshing its model lineup with the so-called Neue Klasse, a digital production platform that will be the basis for its new generation of cars. The first model of the new range will debut at the end of this year with more than 40 new and updated vehicles set to be launched by 2027. A new fuel-cell electric vehicle powered by hydrogen will also be launched in 2028.

It spent over 18 billion euros ($19.54 billion) last year on research and development and capital expenditure linked to new products and facilities, but that marked the peak in its spending plans so expenditure will fall this year, it said.

The company said its new BMW 5 Series, BMW X3, renewed Mini range and BMW 2 Series Gran Coupe will be fully available this year. This, coupled with stabilizing inflation and falling interest rates, should lead to car demand rising slightly, it said.

BMW expects a solid market development in the U.S. and growth in Europe driven by electrified vehicles while China will remain tough.

The company reported an earnings before interest and tax margin at its automotive unit of 6.3% in 2024, down from 9.8% in 2023. The margin was in line with company guidance that forecast a figure in the bottom half of a 6%-7% range. The company had previously downgraded its margin assumption as a recall of cars to fix a braking issue and muted demand in China dented profit.

In the fourth quarter, the margin fell to 5.5% from 8.5% a year earlier.

Group EBIT fell to 11.51 billion euros in 2024 from 18.48 billion euros. A company-compiled consensus had seen EBIT at 12.18 billion euros.

Revenue fell 8.4% to 142.38 billion euros.

BMW cut its dividend to 4.30 euros from 6 euros it paid out for 2023, slightly below the 4.33 euros expected by analysts.

At its annual meeting it will request authorization to buy back additional shares amounting to 10% of the existing share capital within the next five years.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Valmet Bags Order for Press Roll in Germany
data.description:
Valmet secured an order to deliver a press roll for the TwinRoll wash press at Mercer International's Rosenthal pulp mill in Germany.

The replacement press roll will ensure the availability and performance of the machine as well as boost its lifetime, the Finnish pulp and paper technology group said Friday.

Valmet will deliver the press roll to the Canadian market pulp producer during the second quarter of 2026.
